old_claim.note: Mud Brick (Sun-Dried) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Kiln-fired bricks are a fired variant within the same clay-brick building tradition that includes earlier sun-dried adobe bricks.

source_shape.source_locator: The French Ministry of Culture glossary describes Mesopotamian bricks as clay mixed with temper, initially hand-modelled in the Neolithic, later sun-dried adobe or kiln-fired bricks.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source places sun-dried adobe and kiln-fired bricks in one Mesopotamian clay-brick technology sequence.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The edge is retained as an enabling lineage from unfired brick-making to fired brick-making, with chronology supplied by the same glossary.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: mud_brick_sun_dried remains an enabling prerequisite for fired_bricks_early_kilns.
would_reject_if: The fired-brick node is later rescoped to general kiln technology rather than clay building bricks.
A stronger source shows kiln-fired bricks did not develop from or alongside sun-dried clay-brick practice.
A separate clay_brick_forms node is added and should carry the lineage instead.
short_reason: Kiln-fired bricks are source-backed as a later fired variant of clay-brick practice.
